title: Supply Planner
description: Master the full planning process — from S&OP and S&OE through daily execution — then understand how Planning Software and ERP support each step of the way.

keyAreas: The end-to-end operating model: what S&OP, S&OE, and execution look like and how they connect
Who is in the planning team and how supply planning fits into the broader organisation
Why data quality matters and what poor data costs in a planning context
What ERP is and its role in the system landscape alongside planning software
S&OP and S&OE process cycles, key decisions, outputs, and cross-functional dependencies
Planning software data objects, the network model, and demand/supply/inventory logic
ERP data model and the flow of master and transactional data into and out of planning
RASCI accountability across demand, supply, and executive S&OP reviews
Data types, quality standards, and the end-to-end data model from source to plan
Scenario planning fundamentals and how all systems integrate end-to-end
Running demand forecasting, supply planning, inventory, resource, and S&OP review meetings
Executing S&OE monitoring: tracking the near-term plan, managing exceptions, and the integrated review
Daily execution: order prioritisation, actuals capture, and feeding outcomes back to planning
Hands-on planning software: simulations, scenario runs, and reading plan output
ERP workflows: creating orders, goods receipt and issue, and navigating ERP screens
Applying safety stock policy, service level targets, and allocation rules day-to-day
Governance and escalation: how planning exceptions are managed and decisions are surfaced
KPI ownership: which metrics you own, how they're reviewed, and how to steer on them
Data governance: ownership, definitions, and maintaining a single source of truth
Integration patterns: how ERP, planning software, and other systems stay in sync
